NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt, They have done abominable works, There is none who does good.
NKJV.2: The LORD looks down from heaven upon the children of men, To see if there are any who understand, who seek God.
NKJV.3: They have all turned aside, They have together become corrupt; There is none who does good, No, not one.
NKJV.4: Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge, Who eat up my people as they eat bread, And do not call on the LORD?
NKJV.5: There they are in great fear, For God is with the generation of the righteous.
NKJV.6: You shame the counsel of the poor, But the LORD is his refuge.
NKJV.7: Oh, that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion! When the LORD brings back the captivity of His people, Let Jacob rejoice and Israel be glad.

ESV.1: The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt, they do abominable deeds; there is none who does good.
ESV.2: The LORD looks down from heaven on the children of man, to see if there are any who understand,  who seek after God.
ESV.3: They have all turned aside; together they have become corrupt; there is none who does good, not even one.
ESV.4: Have they no knowledge, all the evildoers who eat up my people as they eat bread and do not call upon the LORD?
ESV.5: There they are in great terror, for God is with the generation of the righteous.
ESV.6: You would shame the plans of the poor, but  the LORD is his refuge.
ESV.7: Oh, that salvation for Israel would come out of Zion! When the LORD restores the fortunes of his people, let Jacob rejoice, let Israel be glad.

NASB.1: For the choir director. [A Psalm] of David. The fool has said in his heart, 'There is no God.' They are corrupt, they have committed abominable deeds; There is no one who does good.
NASB.2: The LORD has looked down from heaven upon the sons of men To see if there are any who understand, Who seek after God.
NASB.3: They have all turned aside, together they have become corrupt; There is no one who does good, not even one.
NASB.4: Do all the workers of wickedness not know, Who eat up my people [as] they eat bread, [And] do not call upon the Lord?
NASB.5: There they are in great dread, For God is with the righteous generation.
NASB.6: You would put to shame the counsel of the afflicted, But the LORD is his refuge.
NASB.7: Oh, that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion! When the LORD restores His captive people, Jacob will rejoice, Israel will be glad.

NLT.1:
Only fools say in their hearts,
“There is no God.”
They are corrupt, and their actions are evil;
not one of them does good!
NLT.2:
The LORD looks down from heaven
on the entire human race;
he looks to see if anyone is truly wise,
if anyone seeks God.
NLT.3:
But no, all have turned away;
all have become corrupt.
No one does good,
not a single one!
NLT.4:
Will those who do evil never learn?
They eat up my people like bread
and wouldn’t think of praying to the LORD.
NLT.5:
Terror will grip them,
for God is with those who obey him.
NLT.6:
The wicked frustrate the plans of the oppressed,
but the LORD will protect his people.
NLT.7:
Who will come from Mount Zion to rescue Israel?
When the LORD restores his people,
Jacob will shout with joy, and Israel will rejoice.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. By David. The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt. They have done abominable deeds. There is no one who does good.
WEB.2: Yahweh looked down from heaven on the children of men, to see if there were any who understood, who sought after God.
WEB.3: They have all gone aside. They have together become corrupt. There is no one who does good, no, not one.
WEB.4: Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge, who eat up my people as they eat bread, and don’t call on Yahweh?
WEB.5: There they were in great fear, for God is in the generation of the righteous.
WEB.6: You frustrate the plan of the poor, because Yahweh is his refuge.
WEB.7: Oh that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion! When Yahweh restores the fortunes of his people, then Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

CSB17.1:

They are corrupt; they do vile deeds.

to see if there is one who is wise,

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. The [spiritually ignorant] fool has said in his heart, "There is no God."
They are corrupt, they have committed repulsive and unspeakable deeds;
There is no one who does good.
AMP.2:
The LORD has looked down from heaven upon the children of men
To see if there are any who understand (act wisely),
Who [truly] seek after God, [longing for His wisdom and guidance].
AMP.3:
They have all turned aside, together they have become corrupt;
There is no one who does good, not even one.
AMP.4:
Have all the workers of wickedness and injustice no knowledge,
Who eat up my people as they eat bread,
And do not call upon the LORD?
AMP.5:
There they tremble with great fear,
For God is with the [consistently] righteous generation.
AMP.6:
You [evildoers] shamefully plan against the poor,
But the LORD is his safe refuge.
AMP.7:
Oh, that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion!
When the LORD restores His captive people,
Then Jacob will rejoice, Israel will be glad.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Bilious and bloated, they gas,
"God is gone."
Their words are poison gas,
fouling the air; they poison
Rivers and skies;
thistles are their cash crop.
MSG.2:
God sticks his head out of heaven.
He looks around.
He's looking for someone not stupid—
one man, even, God-expectant,
just one God-ready woman.
MSG.3:
He comes up empty. A string
of zeros. Useless, unshepherded
Sheep, taking turns pretending
to be Shepherd.
The ninety and nine
follow their fellow.
MSG.4:
Don't they know anything,
all these impostors?
Don't they know
they can't get away with this—
Treating people like a fast-food meal
over which they're too busy to pray?
MSG.5:
Night is coming for them, and nightmares,
for God takes the side of victims.
MSG.6:
Do you think you can mess
with the dreams of the poor?
You can't, for God
makes their dreams come true.
MSG.7:
Is there anyone around to save Israel?
Yes. God is around; God turns life around.
Turned-around Jacob skips rope,
turned-around Israel sings laughter.

NIV.1:
Psalm 14
For the director of music. Of David.
The fool says in his heart,
“There is no God.”
They are corrupt, their deeds are vile;
there is no one who does good.
NIV.2:
The Lord looks down from heaven
on the sons of men
to see if there are any who understand,
any who seek God.
NIV.3:
All have turned aside,
they have together become corrupt;
there is no one who does good,
not even one.
NIV.4:
Will evildoers never learn—
those who devour my people as men eat bread
and who do not call on the Lord?
NIV.5:
There they are, overwhelmed with dread,
for God is present in the company of the righteous.
NIV.6:
You evildoers frustrate the plans of the poor,
but the Lord is their refuge.
NIV.7:
Oh, that salvation for Israel would come out of Zion!
When the Lord restores the fortunes of his people,
let Jacob rejoice and Israel be glad!

NRSVCE.1:
Fools say in their hearts, “There is no God.”
They are corrupt, they do abominable deeds;
there is no one who does good.
NRSVCE.2:
The LORD looks down from heaven on humankind
to see if there are any who are wise,
who seek after God.
NRSVCE.3:
They have all gone astray, they are all alike perverse;
there is no one who does good,
no, not one.
NRSVCE.4:
Have they no knowledge, all the evildoers
who eat up my people as they eat bread,
and do not call upon the LORD?
NRSVCE.5:
There they shall be in great terror,
for God is with the company of the righteous.
NRSVCE.6:
You would confound the plans of the poor,
but the LORD is their refuge.
NRSVCE.7:
O that deliverance for Israel would come from Zion!
When the LORD restores the fortunes of his people,
Jacob will rejoice; Israel will be glad.

NET.1: Fools say to themselves, “There is no God.” They sin and commit evil deeds; none of them does what is right.
NET.2: The LORD looks down from heaven at the human race, to see if there is anyone who is wise and seeks God.
NET.3: Everyone rejects God; they are all morally corrupt. None of them does what is right, not even one!
NET.4: All those who behave wickedly do not understand — those who devour my people as if they were eating bread, and do not call out to the LORD.
NET.5: They are absolutely terrified, for God defends the godly.
NET.6: You want to humiliate the oppressed, even though the LORD is their shelter.
NET.7: I wish the deliverance of Israel would come from Zion! When the LORD restores the well-being of his people, may Jacob rejoice, may Israel be happy!
